negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: DKMS RPC capability validation requires HttpCall, but DKMS wallet/fee pricing uses a fixed DKMS executor fee rather than http_executor_fee(input_len, 20).
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-precompile-addresses/src/lib.rs maps DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE to HttpCall for executor registration.
crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs uses DKMS_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI and DKMS_OUTPUT_SIZE_BYTES in calculate_dkms_fees, not http_executor_fee(input_len, 20).
crates/ritual-async-fees/src/wallet.rs uses dkms_escrow_lockup for wallet checks.

notes: Because the invariant requires both pricing and capability behavior, the fixed-fee DKMS implementation is insufficient.
rationale: The commit classifies DKMS as a simple async precompile with small fixed output for fee purposes and as an HttpCall-capability workload for executor registration. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: DKMS key commitments must use HTTP-style wallet executor fee pricing with http_executor_fee(input_len, 20), and RPC executor registration validation must require Capability::HttpCall for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE.
suggested_falsification_tests: For DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE, assert wallet fee verification calculates executor_fee with http_executor_fee(input_len, 20), and assert executor registration validation accepts an executor with HttpCall capability and rejects one lacking it under this capability rule.
summary: DKMS HTTP-style fee pricing is absent, although the HttpCall capability mapping is present.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE, assert wallet fee verification calculates executor_fee with http_executor_fee(input_len, 20), and assert executor registration validation accepts an executor with HttpCall capability and rejects one lacking it under this capability rule.
title: DKMS uses HTTP-style fees and capability
